top_recommendations: Quantum-vulnerable key exchange: migrate from ECDHE to ML-KEM-768 or ML-KEM-1024
Enable TLS 1.3 and configure PQC key exchange (ML-KEM-768) on your server.
Obtain a PQC-signed certificate using ML-DSA or SLH-DSA from a supporting CA.
Consider hybrid key exchange (X25519+ML-KEM-768) as a transitional step.

findings.title: Quantum-vulnerable key exchange
findings.description: Key exchange 'ECDHE' is vulnerable to Shor's algorithm. A sufficiently powerful quantum computer could break this.

findings.title: TLS 1.2 detected — upgrade recommended
findings.description: TLS 1.2 does not support PQC key exchange natively. Upgrade to TLS 1.3.

quantum_vulnerable_algorithms.name: ECDHE
quantum_vulnerable_algorithms.notes: Uses DH, which is vulnerable to Shor's algorithm.

quantum_vulnerable_algorithms.name: ECDHE-RSA-AES256-GCM-SHA384
quantum_vulnerable_algorithms.notes: Uses DH, which is vulnerable to Shor's algorithm.

pqc_assessments.details: No quantum-safe algorithms detected.  This endpoint is vulnerable to future quantum attacks (Harvest Now, Decrypt Later).

recommendations_detailed.title: Migrate to Post-Quantum Key Exchange
recommendations_detailed.description: Replace current key exchange mechanism with ML-KEM-768 (FIPS 203). Use hybrid X25519+ML-KEM-768 as a transitional step for backward compatibility.

server_configs.Apache (OpenSSL 3.5+):
# Apache — Enable TLS 1.3
SSLProtocol -all +TLSv1.3
SSLCipherSuite TLS_AES_256_GCM_SHA384:TLS_CHACHA20_POLY1305_SHA256
SSLOpenSSLConfCmd Groups X25519MLKEM768:X25519:secp384r1
server_configs.HAProxy:
# HAProxy — TLS 1.3 with PQC groups
bind *:443 ssl crt /etc/haproxy/certs/ alpn h2,http/1.1
ssl-default-bind-ciphers TLS_AES_256_GCM_SHA384:TLS_CHACHA20_POLY1305_SHA256
ssl-default-bind-options ssl-min-ver TLSv1.3
ssl-default-bind-curves X25519MLKEM768:X25519:secp384r1
server_configs.AWS ALB:
# AWS ALB — Quantum-safe cipher policy
# Use the 'ELBSecurityPolicy-TLS13-1-3-2021-06' or later policy
# Enable X25519MLKEM768 group in the ALB listener configuration
# Reference: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/elasticloadbalancing/latest/application/

recommendations_detailed.title: Upgrade to TLS 1.3
recommendations_detailed.description: TLS 1.3 is required for PQC cipher suites and provides improved security with mandatory forward secrecy. Disable TLS 1.0, 1.1, and consider deprecating 1.2.

recommendations_detailed.title: Implement Crypto Agility
recommendations_detailed.description: Design systems to easily swap cryptographic algorithms without major code changes. This ensures smooth future transitions as PQC standards evolve.

recommendations_detailed.title: Establish PQC Migration Timeline
recommendations_detailed.description: Create a phased migration plan aligned with NIST's 2035 deadline for deprecating quantum-vulnerable algorithms. Prioritize high-value endpoints handling financial data.
